verses.text: Pilate scourges Him; the soldiers crown Him with thorns, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' and smite Him. Mock crown. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
Pilate scourges Him; the soldiers crown Him with thorns, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' and smite Him. Mock crown. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
Pilate scourges Him; the soldiers crown Him with thorns, 'Hail, King of the Jews,' and smite Him. Mock crown. They jeer the kingship that is truly His.
'Behold the man'; I find no fault; they cry, Crucify, 'he made himself the Son of God.' True charge. They condemn Him for the very truth of who He is.
'Behold the man'; I find no fault; they cry, Crucify, 'he made himself the Son of God.' True charge. They condemn Him for the very truth of who He is.
'Behold the man'; I find no fault; they cry, Crucify, 'he made himself the Son of God.' True charge. They condemn Him for the very truth of who He is.
'Behold the man'; I find no fault; they cry, Crucify, 'he made himself the Son of God.' True charge. They condemn Him for the very truth of who He is.
Pilate fears; 'thou couldest have no power... except it were given thee from above.' Power from God. Even Pilate's authority is from heaven.
Pilate fears; 'thou couldest have no power... except it were given thee from above.' Power from God. Even Pilate's authority is from heaven.
Pilate fears; 'thou couldest have no power... except it were given thee from above.' Power from God. Even Pilate's authority is from heaven.
Pilate fears; 'thou couldest have no power... except it were given thee from above.' Power from God. Even Pilate's authority is from heaven.
If thou let Him go, 'thou art not Caesar's friend'; Pilate brings Him forth, 'Behold your King.' Pressured. Fear of Caesar overrides justice.
If thou let Him go, 'thou art not Caesar's friend'; Pilate brings Him forth, 'Behold your King.' Pressured. Fear of Caesar overrides justice.
If thou let Him go, 'thou art not Caesar's friend'; Pilate brings Him forth, 'Behold your King.' Pressured. Fear of Caesar overrides justice.
'We have no king but Caesar'; he delivers Him to be crucified. Christ denied. They disown their King to keep Caesar.
'We have no king but Caesar'; he delivers Him to be crucified. Christ denied. They disown their King to keep Caesar.
He bears His cross to Golgotha, where they crucify Him between two others. The cross. Numbered with transgressors, He is lifted up.
He bears His cross to Golgotha, where they crucify Him between two others. The cross. Numbered with transgressors, He is lifted up.
Pilate writes, 'JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS'; 'What I have written I have written.' Unwitting truth. The title proclaims what the priests deny.
Pilate writes, 'JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS'; 'What I have written I have written.' Unwitting truth. The title proclaims what the priests deny.
Pilate writes, 'JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS'; 'What I have written I have written.' Unwitting truth. The title proclaims what the priests deny.
Pilate writes, 'JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS'; 'What I have written I have written.' Unwitting truth. The title proclaims what the priests deny.
They part His garments and cast lots for His coat, fulfilling Scripture. Foretold detail. Even His clothing fulfils the word.
They part His garments and cast lots for His coat, fulfilling Scripture. Foretold detail. Even His clothing fulfils the word.
His mother stands by; 'Woman, behold thy son'; 'Behold thy mother'; the disciple takes her home. Tender care. Dying, He provides for His mother.
His mother stands by; 'Woman, behold thy son'; 'Behold thy mother'; the disciple takes her home. Tender care. Dying, He provides for His mother.
His mother stands by; 'Woman, behold thy son'; 'Behold thy mother'; the disciple takes her home. Tender care. Dying, He provides for His mother.
'I thirst'; He receives vinegar, says, 'It is finished,' and gives up the ghost. The finished work. The whole work of salvation is accomplished.
'I thirst'; He receives vinegar, says, 'It is finished,' and gives up the ghost. The finished work. The whole work of salvation is accomplished.
'I thirst'; He receives vinegar, says, 'It is finished,' and gives up the ghost. The finished work. The whole work of salvation is accomplished.
They break the others' legs, but not His; a soldier pierces His side, and blood and water flow. Not a bone broken. The Passover Lamb is fulfilled in Him.
They break the others' legs, but not His; a soldier pierces His side, and blood and water flow. Not a bone broken. The Passover Lamb is fulfilled in Him.
They break the others' legs, but not His; a soldier pierces His side, and blood and water flow. Not a bone broken. The Passover Lamb is fulfilled in Him.
They break the others' legs, but not His; a soldier pierces His side, and blood and water flow. Not a bone broken. The Passover Lamb is fulfilled in Him.
The witness bare record; 'they shall look on him whom they pierced.' Sure testimony. The eyewitness confirms the fulfilled Scriptures.
The witness bare record; 'they shall look on him whom they pierced.' Sure testimony. The eyewitness confirms the fulfilled Scriptures.
The witness bare record; 'they shall look on him whom they pierced.' Sure testimony. The eyewitness confirms the fulfilled Scriptures.
Joseph and Nicodemus take the body, wind it with spices, and lay it in a new tomb. Honoured burial. Secret disciples now come forth boldly.
